Bearing 315

The newsletter of the Cascade Orienteering Club | January-March 2001
www.CascadeOC.org | Page 1

![Logo: A stylized mountain range profile beside a compass rose, with the text "Bearing 315"]

Current COC Officers and Coordinators

__PH_0__ – Dave Tallent, 425-488-3691, dtallent@gtemail.net __PH_1__ – Eric Bone, 206-634-2279, e30ne@yahoo.com Treasurer – Katie Sprugel, 206-365-8742, sprugelk@zgi.com
__PH_3__ – Ardis Dull, 206-364-4062, ardisdull@juno.com Environmental Coordinator – Rick Hood, 425-640-8134, rick@hoodcs.com
Mapping Coordinator – Debbie Newell, 425-488-3691, dnewel@gte.net
Equipment Coordinator – Don Atkinson, 425-488-0270, dona@atmos.washington.edu
__PH_7__ – Debbie Newell Newsletter Editor-Elect - Justin Yeager, 253-709-8232, yeagerjustin@hotmail.com
__PH_9__ – Aaron Charlop, 206-935-8742, charlop@u.washington.edu Publicity/Promotion Coordinator – Eileen Breseman, 425-483-6592, breseman@msn.com
__PH_11__ - Dave Tallent, 425-488-3691, dtallent@gtemail.net Volunteer Coordinator – vacant
Bill Cusworth, 206-271-0652, bill3@halcyon.com
Jan Urban, 425-402-7225, urbanj@molecumetics.com

\ Executive Board Member*

Membership Report

Welcome to the following new and returning club members:

Anthony Amort, Brett Aniballi, Laura Barnes, Laura Bendit, Stuart Bradshaw, Breseman family, John Crawford, Donald Denbo, Cindy Dennis, S Donald, Robert Donovan, Todd Drobnick, Stacey Dufrene, Tomas Eidsmo, Marie Emerson, Robert Ferguson, Hans Frederick, Jerry Graham, Rich Hummez, Kent Jameson, Cam Jones, DJ Jones, Ken Knapp, Jennifer Knowles, Jeff Leer, James Leise, Olav Liland, Ben Lischner, Chris Lum, Arne Lund, Todd Matthews, Bo McFadden, Jon Mercer, Barbara Mungia, Ben Nachtrieb, Debbie Newell, Jenny Pearson, Scott Phillips, Katherine Pitt, Kevin Quiberson, Erika Rado, Derek Richert, Michael Schuh, James Siscel, David Tallent, Jan Urban, Chuck & Jan Ward, and Marshall Will.

New Bearing 315 Editor

I am happy to report that, beginning with the next issue of Bearing 315, we will have a new editor. Justin Yeager has agreed to serve in this capacity, so please help him out by sending him interesting articles.

Election Results

As expected, all 5 candidates for COC's Board of Directors were elected unanimously at our Annual Meeting (plus one mail-in vote) on November 19. Your BOD for 2001 is: Don Atkinson, Eric Bone, Bill Cusworth, Debbie Newell, and Jan Urban. The Board of Directors will select a President, Vice President, Secretary, and Treasurer and the President will then appoint the Coordinator positions.

Adventure Racer Speaks

As part of the entertainment at the Annual Meeting, we were treated to an informal talk by Marc Peck of Team Cascade, who (with his 3 teammates) completed the Eco Challenge in Borneo last August. Marc is hoping to participate in this year's Eco Challenge in New Zealand. For more information about the Eco Challenge, visit their web page at www.ecochallenge.com.

Volunteer Coordinator

If you like to talk on the phone, we have the perfect job for you. We always need people to help at meets and an organized, efficient way to get them. The Volunteer Coordinator is a critical position in the club and one that we have done without for too long. If you'd like more information about this key position, please contact Debbie Newell (425-488-3691, dnewel@gte.net).

Meet Volunteers Sought

As you've probably noticed, each of our meets requires several volunteers in order to ensure it runs like a well-oiled machine and we are always looking for people to help out. If you don't feel confident enough to try directing a meet or setting courses, offer to help out at the registration, start, or finish areas. All of these jobs require minimal training and only take an hour or two at the meet. To volunteer, contact Debbie Newell (425-488-3691, dnewel@gte.net).

The Ultimate Orienteer Series

The second annual Ultimate Orienteer series begins in March. This year, again, we will offer 4 different orienteering styles: Short-O, Score-O, Classic-O, and Long-O. You will receive points based on your finish placing, and your best 3 scores will be added together to determine the male and female “Ultimate Orienteer.” See the schedule page for Ultimate Orienteer events.

Presidential Farewell

[After serving as Cascade's president for the past 2 years, Dave Tallent has decided to call it quits. During his tenure, Dave has set courses and directed meets more than 30 times. He has also been WIOL director for 7 of the past 10 years and was president of Sammamish Orienteering Club from 1988-1998.]

It has been really rewarding helping to guide “O” in the Pacific NW; to see the WIOL program grow from a few dozen students to over 100; to see the tremendous growth in meet participation at local meets from as few as 20 to over 200 and a growth in Cascade’s membership (currently 179, with another 80 just chomping at the bit to renew–hint, hint!). Cascade has a solid annual program with the highly successful Winter O' Series and the fledgling Ultimate Orienteer Series.

This growth is due in large part to a core group of COC members who have worked hard to make it happen. At the risk of leaving someone out, I would like to thank the following [continued on Page 8]

Adventure Run - St. Edward State Park - November 19

Meet Directors: Bill Cusworth, Rick Hood
Course: Bill Cusworth
Registration: Bill Cusworth
On-site Registration: Sophia Hayes
Finish: Robert Miller, Sandy Kish Miller, Sophia Hayes
Setup: Saul Kinderis, Kean Williams, Uli Steidl
Refreshments: Bruce McAlister
Course Pickup: Uli Steidl, Scott McCoubrey, Debbie Newell, Dave Tallent, Dan Hayes, Sophia Hayes

105 runners participated in the 3rd annual Adventure Run at St. Edward State Park. The morning fog lifted just before the 10am mass start, yielding perfect weather for a November race. The race consisted almost entirely of trails and grass which was unusually firm due to the recent lack of rain. The course had 10 mandatory checkpoints leading to a variety of route choices taken.

Dan Hayes was the repeat winner from last year taking 2 minutes off last year’s winning time. The finish line menu of chili, soup, cornbread, and fruit was well-received by all the participants. Thanks go to Scott McCoubrey of Capitol Hill FootZone for sponsoring the race.

Frequent O'er Card

![Image: A promotional "Frequent O'er Card" showing a punch card with ten $5 boxes, adorned with floral decorations and reading "This card may be used at any event hosted by the Cascade Orienteering Club"]

You can now prepay your entry to Cascade events and get a bonus, too. For $50, you will get a card like this. To pay for an event, just have one of the boxes punched. After you've used up all 10 boxes, give the card to the registrar for a bonus $5 credit. To purchase a card, contact Debbie Newell (425-488-3691, dnewel@gte.net). These cards are only valid at Cascade O' Club events.

(Page 8)

(Presidential Farewell - continued from Page 1)

people for their continuing efforts to make our club successful: Don Atkinson, Don Denbo, Ardis Dull, Debbie Newell, and Jan Urban.

I can’t say enough about these club members, who have given our meets such a professional look. However, they too need a rest! It’s time for new blood. Please offer your help at future meets. COC needs you, and the personal satisfaction and rewards are great.

I will continue my involvement in orienteering by serving as Land Use Coordinator for 2001, but am anxious to train someone new.

My first love will always be orienteering, but I wish to devote more time to training for the Hawaii Ironman triathlon and adventure racing.

Best wishes to you all and see you in the woods.

Dave Tallent

Bearing 315
c/o Cascade Orienteering Club
P.O. Box 31375
Seattle WA 98103

First Class

block_index.text: The newsletter of the Cascade Orienteering Club | January-March 2001 www.CascadeOC.org | Page 1
![Logo: A stylized mountain range profile beside a compass rose, with the text "Bearing 315"]

Lame Duck President – Dave Tallent, 425-488-3691, dtallent@gtemail.net Vice President/Training Coordinator – Eric Bone, 206-634-2279, e30ne@yahoo.com Treasurer – Katie Sprugel, 206-365-8742, sprugelk@zgi.com Secretary – Ardis Dull, 206-364-4062, ardisdull@juno.com Environmental Coordinator – Rick Hood, 425-640-8134, rick@hoodcs.com Mapping Coordinator – Debbie Newell, 425-488-3691, dnewel@gte.net Equipment Coordinator – Don Atkinson, 425-488-0270, dona@atmos.washington.edu The Accidental Newsletter Editor – Debbie Newell Newsletter Editor-Elect - Justin Yeager, 253-709-8232, yeagerjustin@hotmail.com Membership Coordinator – Aaron Charlop, 206-935-8742, charlop@u.washington.edu Publicity/Promotion Coordinator – Eileen Breseman, 425-483-6592, breseman@msn.com Land Use Coordinator - Dave Tallent, 425-488-3691, dtallent@gtemail.net Volunteer Coordinator – vacant Bill Cusworth, 206-271-0652, bill3@halcyon.com Jan Urban, 425-402-7225, urbanj@molecumetics.com
Welcome to the following new and returning club members:

Anthony Amort, Brett Aniballi, Laura Barnes, Laura Bendit, Stuart Bradshaw, Breseman family, John Crawford, Donald Denbo, Cindy Dennis, S Donald, Robert Donovan, Todd Drobnick, Stacey Dufrene, Tomas Eidsmo, Marie Emerson, Robert Ferguson, Hans Frederick, Jerry Graham, Rich Hummez, Kent Jameson, Cam Jones, DJ Jones, Ken Knapp, Jennifer Knowles, Jeff Leer, James Leise, Olav Liland, Ben Lischner, Chris Lum, Arne Lund, Todd Matthews, Bo McFadden, Jon Mercer, Barbara Mungia, Ben Nachtrieb, Debbie Newell, Jenny Pearson, Scott Phillips, Katherine Pitt, Kevin Quiberson, Erika Rado, Derek Richert, Michael Schuh, James Siscel, David Tallent, Jan Urban, Chuck & Jan Ward, and Marshall Will.

I am happy to report that, beginning with the next issue of Bearing 315, we will have a new editor. Justin Yeager has agreed to serve in this capacity, so please help him out by sending him interesting articles.

As expected, all 5 candidates for COC's Board of Directors were elected unanimously at our Annual Meeting (plus one mail-in vote) on November 19. Your BOD for 2001 is: Don Atkinson, Eric Bone, Bill Cusworth, Debbie Newell, and Jan Urban. The Board of Directors will select a President, Vice President, Secretary, and Treasurer and the President will then appoint the Coordinator positions.

As part of the entertainment at the Annual Meeting, we were treated to an informal talk by Marc Peck of Team Cascade, who (with his 3 teammates) completed the Eco Challenge in Borneo last August. Marc is hoping to participate in this year's Eco Challenge in New Zealand. For more information about the Eco Challenge, visit their web page at www.ecochallenge.com.

If you like to talk on the phone, we have the perfect job for you. We always need people to help at meets and an organized, efficient way to get them. The Volunteer Coordinator is a critical position in the club and one that we have done without for too long. If you'd like more information about this key position, please contact Debbie Newell (425-488-3691, dnewel@gte.net).

As you've probably noticed, each of our meets requires several volunteers in order to ensure it runs like a well-oiled machine and we are always looking for people to help out. If you don't feel confident enough to try directing a meet or setting courses, offer to help out at the registration, start, or finish areas. All of these jobs require minimal training and only take an hour or two at the meet. To volunteer, contact Debbie Newell (425-488-3691, dnewel@gte.net).

The second annual Ultimate Orienteer series begins in March. This year, again, we will offer 4 different orienteering styles: Short-O, Score-O, Classic-O, and Long-O. You will receive points based on your finish placing, and your best 3 scores will be added together to determine the male and female “Ultimate Orienteer.” See the schedule page for Ultimate Orienteer events.

[After serving as Cascade's president for the past 2 years, Dave Tallent has decided to call it quits. During his tenure, Dave has set courses and directed meets more than 30 times. He has also been WIOL director for 7 of the past 10 years and was president of Sammamish Orienteering Club from 1988-1998.]

It has been really rewarding helping to guide “O” in the Pacific NW; to see the WIOL program grow from a few dozen students to over 100; to see the tremendous growth in meet participation at local meets from as few as 20 to over 200 and a growth in Cascade’s membership (currently 179, with another 80 just chomping at the bit to renew–hint, hint!). Cascade has a solid annual program with the highly successful Winter O' Series and the fledgling Ultimate Orienteer Series.
This growth is due in large part to a core group of COC members who have worked hard to make it happen. At the risk of leaving someone out, I would like to thank the following [continued on Page 8]

Adventure Run - St. Edward State Park - November 19

Meet Directors: Bill Cusworth, Rick Hood Course: Bill Cusworth Registration: Bill Cusworth On-site Registration: Sophia Hayes Finish: Robert Miller, Sandy Kish Miller, Sophia Hayes Setup: Saul Kinderis, Kean Williams, Uli Steidl Refreshments: Bruce McAlister Course Pickup: Uli Steidl, Scott McCoubrey, Debbie Newell, Dave Tallent, Dan Hayes, Sophia Hayes

105 runners participated in the 3rd annual Adventure Run at St. Edward State Park. The morning fog lifted just before the 10am mass start, yielding perfect weather for a November race. The race consisted almost entirely of trails and grass which was unusually firm due to the recent lack of rain. The course had 10 mandatory checkpoints leading to a variety of route choices taken.

Dan Hayes was the repeat winner from last year taking 2 minutes off last year’s winning time. The finish line menu of chili, soup, cornbread, and fruit was well-received by all the participants. Thanks go to Scott McCoubrey of Capitol Hill FootZone for sponsoring the race.

![Image: A promotional "Frequent O'er Card" showing a punch card with ten $5 boxes, adorned with floral decorations and reading "This card may be used at any event hosted by the Cascade Orienteering Club"]

You can now prepay your entry to Cascade events and get a bonus, too. For $50, you will get a card like this. To pay for an event, just have one of the boxes punched. After you've used up all 10 boxes, give the card to the registrar for a bonus $5 credit. To purchase a card, contact Debbie Newell (425-488-3691, dnewel@gte.net). These cards are only valid at Cascade O' Club events.

people for their continuing efforts to make our club successful: Don Atkinson, Don Denbo, Ardis Dull, Debbie Newell, and Jan Urban.

I can’t say enough about these club members, who have given our meets such a professional look. However, they too need a rest! It’s time for new blood. Please offer your help at future meets. COC needs you, and the personal satisfaction and rewards are great.
I will continue my involvement in orienteering by serving as Land Use Coordinator for 2001, but am anxious to train someone new.
My first love will always be orienteering, but I wish to devote more time to training for the Hawaii Ironman triathlon and adventure racing.
Best wishes to you all and see you in the woods.
Bearing 315 c/o Cascade Orienteering Club P.O. Box 31375 Seattle WA 98103
